name: Tahmina Watson
description: Tahmina Watson is a nationally recognized immigration attorney, author, and advocate who has dedicated her career to helping entrepreneurs, investors, and businesses navigate U.S. immigration law.
barAdmissions: U.S. District Court, Western District of Washington
awards: Puget Sound Business Journal Women of Influence
Named Top Immigration Lawyer by Seattle Met
credentials: 15+ years of U.S. immigration law practice
Author of The Startup Visa (two editions)
Host of Tahmina Talks Immigration® Podcast
Featured in New York Times, Forbes, Bloomberg, The Guardian, NPR
Legal Foundation of Washington board member
bio: Tahmina Watson is the founder and managing attorney of Watson Immigration Law, a Seattle-based immigration law firm she established to serve entrepreneurs, investors, and multinational companies navigating the U.S. immigration system.

Born in Bangladesh and educated in the United Kingdom, Tahmina brings a deeply personal understanding of the immigrant experience to her practice. She earned her LL.B. and LL.M. in International Commercial Law from the University of Northumbria before building her career in U.S. immigration law.

Tahmina has become one of the country's leading voices on startup and entrepreneur immigration. Her book, The Startup Visa, is considered essential reading for international founders seeking a U.S. immigration pathway. She has testified before Congress, spoken at SelectUSA summits, and been quoted extensively in major publications including the New York Times, Forbes, Bloomberg Law, The Guardian, and NPR.

She is the host of Tahmina Talks Immigration®, a podcast featuring conversations with policymakers, attorneys, entrepreneurs, and advocates. The show explores the intersection of immigration law, entrepreneurship, and policy.

Tahmina practices across all employment-based visa categories with particular depth in EB-5, E-2, H-1B, O-1, and L-1 visas. She represents clients from over 50 countries and is known for her innovative, strategic approach to complex cases.
